source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/02_ADD (HAD1417MCW)/▶ 국내 제조인증/3. HAD1717MCW 모델 추가_210723/성적서, 소프트웨어 밸리데이션/Firmware SW Validation/Software 62304 checklist_Ver 20200921.pdf

sourceSummary.oneLine: Checklist text for IEC 62304 medical device software lifecycle process requirements, including risk management, software safety classification, and change test documentation.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports firmware software validation documentation for domestic manufacturing certification/model addition records.
keyPoints: Manufacturer must apply a risk management process complying with ISO 14971.
Software systems are assigned safety classes A, B, or C according to possible effects on patient, operator, or other people.
Software safety class assignments and rationales are documented in the risk management file.
Testing, retesting, or regression testing after changes must include results, anomalies, software version, configurations, tools, date tested, and tester identification.

entities.name: Common Sense Systems, Inc.
entities.evidence: This document contains confidential information proprietary to Common Sense Systems, Inc.

entities.name: MANUFACTURER
entities.evidence: The MANUFACTURER of MEDICAL DEVICE SOFTWARE shall demonstrate the ability to provide MEDICAL DEVICE SOFTWARE...

concepts.name: IEC 62304 Checklist
concepts.evidence: Reference Software Lifecycle Process Applicable for IEC 62304 Checklist

concepts.name: Risk management process
concepts.evidence: The MANUFACTURER shall apply a RISK MANAGEMENT PROCESS complying with ISO 14971.

concepts.name: Software safety classification
concepts.evidence: The MANUFACTURER shall assign to each SOFTWARE SYSTEM a software safety class (A, B or C)...

concepts.name: Risk management file
concepts.evidence: The MANUFACTURER shall document the software safety class assigned to each SOFTWARE SYSTEM in the RISK MANAGEMENT FILE.

concepts.name: Regression testing after software changes
concepts.evidence: When testing, retesting, or REGRESSION TESTING SOFTWARE ITEMS and SYSTEMS following a change...

evidence.claim: The document is an IEC 62304 checklist for a reference software lifecycle process.
evidence.evidenceText: Reference Software Lifecycle Process Applicable for IEC 62304 Checklist

evidence.claim: The manufacturer must apply a risk management process complying with ISO 14971.
evidence.evidenceText: The MANUFACTURER shall apply a RISK MANAGEMENT PROCESS complying with ISO 14971.

evidence.claim: Medical device software must consistently meet customer requirements and applicable regulatory requirements.
evidence.evidenceText: The MANUFACTURER of MEDICAL DEVICE SOFTWARE shall demonstrate the ability to provide MEDICAL DEVICE SOFTWARE that consistently meets customer requirements and applicable regulatory requirements.

evidence.claim: Software systems are assigned safety classes A, B, or C based on possible effects from hazards.
evidence.evidenceText: The MANUFACTURER shall assign to each SOFTWARE SYSTEM a software safety class (A, B or C) according to the possible effects on the patient, operator or other people resulting from a HAZARD...

evidence.claim: Class A means no injury or damage to health is possible.
evidence.evidenceText: Class A: No injury or damage to health is possible

evidence.claim: Class B means non-serious injury is possible.
evidence.evidenceText: Class B: Non-SERIOUS INJURY is possible

evidence.claim: Class C means death or serious injury is possible.
evidence.evidenceText: Class C: Death or SERIOUS INJURY is possible

evidence.claim: Software safety class assignments must be documented in the risk management file.
evidence.evidenceText: The MANUFACTURER shall document the software safety class assigned to each SOFTWARE SYSTEM in the RISK MANAGEMENT FILE.

evidence.claim: Software items inherit the software safety classification of the original item or system unless a rationale for a different class is documented.
evidence.evidenceText: Such SOFTWARE ITEMS shall inherit the software safety classification of the original SOFTWARE ITEM (or SOFTWARE SYSTEM) unless the MANUFACTURER documents a rationale for classification into a different software safety class.

evidence.claim: After software changes, test documentation must include test results, anomalies, software version, configurations, tools, test date, and tester identification.
evidence.evidenceText: When testing, retesting, or REGRESSION TESTING SOFTWARE ITEMS and SYSTEMS following a change, the MANUFACTURER has included test results... ANOMALIES... VERSION of software tested... relevant hardware and software test configurations... relevant test tools... date tested... identification of the tester...

reviewFlags.reason: Document date is not explicitly stated in the source text; 20200921 appears only as part of the filename/version.
Authority or jurisdiction is provided as metadata signal only and is not stated in the extracted document text.
Product models are derived from sourcePath/productSignal metadata rather than the visible checklist text.
Source text appears to contain OCR or extraction errors such as 'PRIMARY LIFECYCLE PROCESESS' and 'Software Lift Cycle'.
